---
pagetitle: "David Maguire — Quantitative Research"
page-layout: full
toc: false
listing:
  - id: featured
    contents: "research/**/*.qmd"
    type: default
    max-items: 3
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title, description, reading-time, categories]
  - id: latest-notes
    contents: "notes/**/*.qmd"
    type: table
    max-items: 6
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title]
    field-display-names:
      title: "Paper"
---

::: {.hero}
# David Maguire

::: {.lead}
Quantitative researcher working on systematic strategies, risk, and market
microstructure. This site is my open lab notebook — the ideas, equations, and
experiments I'm working through on the way to a PhD. I care most about work that
is *reproducible*, honestly reported, and grounded in the literature.
:::

[Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Reading notes](notes.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
[About & interests](about.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

## Featured research

::: {#featured}
:::

## Latest reading notes

::: {#latest-notes}
:::

## What you'll find here

Longer **research** write-ups pair the maths with runnable code and honest
limitations — including negative results, which I think are the most useful
things to publish. Shorter **notes** are structured annotations of papers I'm
reading. Everything that involves data ships with the code to reproduce it.
